certificate.moduleName: Apple corecrypto Module v12.0 [Apple silicon, Secure Key Store, Hardware, SL2/PHY3]
certificate.description: Apple corecrypto Module v12.0 [Apple silicon, Secure Key Store, Hardware, SL2/PHY3] is a Hardware module implemented as a sub-chip running on a single-chip processor.

services.name: Non- Approved mode
services.description: Non-Approved mode of operation is entered when the module utilizes non-approved security functions in the Table Non-Approved Algorithms Not Allowed in the Approved Mode of Operation.
services.indicator: return a '0' from fips_allowed_mode() for block cipher functions and fips_allowed() for all other services to indicate the executed cryptographic algorithm was non- approved

services.name: User Keybag Services via Mailbox
services.description: Step 1: The module receives User credential and the reference to the class C or A key from the User keybag; Step 2. Obfuscation is performed on the User provided credential resulting into a value called REK.; Step 3. REK is used as a key for the AES KW operation to unwrap the referenced class A or C keys in the user keybag stored in the module; Step 4. Status of unwrapping operation of class keys is returned via
services.cspsAccessed: User - Class A, Class C, Class AK, Class AKU, Class CK, Class CKU in User Keybag (AES keys): W,E - REK: W,E - Authentic ation Credential : W,E

services.input: User creden tial, referen ce to class C/A key from the user keybag

services.name: Generation of Data Encryption Key (DEK)
services.description: Step 1: The module receives the reference to the class C or A key from the user keybag; Step 2: The module generates a new DEK using the DRBG; Step 3: Referenced class C or A key is used to wrap the DEK using AES-KW; Step 4: Wrapped DEK is
services.cspsAccessed: User - Class A, Class C, Class AK, Class AKU, Class CK, Class CKU in User Keybag (AES keys): W,E - Entropy input string: W,E - DRBG
services.indicator: Success returne d from API listed in the custom er proprie tary guidan ce docum ent
services.input: referen ce to class C/A key from the User keybag

services.description: sent out of the module
services.cspsAccessed: seed: W,E - DRBG internal state (V value, Key): W,E - Data Encryptio n Key (DEK) (AES key): G,W,E
services.name: sent out of the module
Keychain DEK service using AK/AKU/AKPU/ CK/CKU class key
services.description: Step 1. The module receives wrapped DEK (that was sent as part of service 3 above) and the pointer to class key AK/AKU/AKPU/ CK/CKU from the user keybag; Step 2. Using the referenced class key, the module unwraps the DEK using AES- KW. If the class key is not available, an error is returned; Step 3. plaintext DEK is sent out to the User. (AS09.16)
services.cspsAccessed: User - Class A, Class C, Class AK, Class AKU, Class CK, Class CKU in User Keybag (AES keys): W,E - Data Encryptio n Key (DEK) (AES key): W,E

services.name: Export Keybag
services.description: Step 1. The module receives reference to a keybag; Step 2: A HMAC key is taken as input based on the hardware specific data for the SKS; Step 3: HMAC value is calculated on the entire referenced keybag that includes encrypted keys; Step 4: HMAC is appended at the end of the keybag; Step 5: keybag with the appended HMAC is output to the User
services.cspsAccessed: User - HMAC key: W,E - Class A, Class C, Class AK, Class AKU, Class CK, Class CKU in User Keybag (AES keys): R,E - Class A, Class C, Class AK, Class AKU, Class CK, Class CKU in Backup Keybag (AES keys): R,E - Class A, Class C, Class AK, Class AKU, Class CK, Class CKU
services.approvedFunctions: HMAC Message Authenticat ion Message Digest
services.indicator: Success returne d from API listed in the custom er proprie tary guidan ce docum ent
services.input: referen ce to a keybag to be export ed

services.name: Encrypt or Decrypt service using Class B Curve 22519 key from any keybag
services.description: shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derived a key which is used doe data encryption or decryption. During encryption operations, the wrapped key and the ephemeral public key is sent to the user
services.roleAccess: Curve 25519 key generation Curve 25519 shared secret generation HKDF RFC5869 AES KW using class D key, keys from Device keybag, keys from iCloud keybag or NVM storage controller key

services.name: Wrap or unwrap service for DEK or keychain using any Curve 22519 key from asymmetric keybag
services.description: shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derived a key which is used to wrap and unwrap DEK or keychain. During wrapping operation, the wrapped key and the ephemeral public key is sent to the user
services.roleAccess: Curve 25519 key generation Curve 25519 shared secret generation HKDF RFC5869 AES KW using class D key, keys from Device keybag, keys from iCloud keybag or NVM storage controller key

services.name: Asymmetric (Ed25519) backup keybag wrap and unwrap

services.roleAccess: Ed25519 Key generation Ed25519 shared secret generation HKDF RFC5869 AES KW using class D key, keys from Device

services.name: Wrap or unwrap service for keychain using DK/DKU/CK/ CKU/AK/AKU/AKPU Ed25519 key from asymmetric keybag
services.description: shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derived a key which is used to wrap and unwrap. The wrapped key and the ephemeral public key is sent to the user
services.roleAccess: Ed25519 Key generation Ed25519 shared secret generation HKDF RFC5869 AES KW using class D key, keys from Device keybag, keys from iCloud keybag or NVM storage controller key

services.name: Obfuscation of User Input Authentication Credential
Error state
services.description: The HMAC-SHA-256 value computed over the module did not match the pre- computed value, OR the computed value in the invoked Conditional CAST did not match the known value. No cryptographic services are provided, and data output is prohibited
services.roleAccess: Pre- operational Firmware Integrity Test failure OR Conditional CAST failure
services.indicator: for Integrity: print statement "FAILED: fipspost_post_integrity" to stdout; for CAST: sprint statement "FAILED:<event>" to stdout (<event> refers to any of the cryptographic functions listed in the Conditional Self- test Table)

portsAndInterfaces.dataThatPasses: Data inputs/outputs are provided through the memory used for mailbox and IPC
portsAndInterfaces.name: Mailbox Memory, IPC channel

portsAndInterfaces.dataThatPasses: Control input which controls the module's operation is provided through the mailbox by the Device OS' kernel and to applications located within the sepOS execution environment through IPC.
portsAndInterfaces.name: Mailbox Memory, IPC channel

portsAndInterfaces.dataThatPasses: Status output is provided in return codes and through messages returned via the mailbox or the IPC. Documentation for each service invocation lists possible return codes. A complete list of all return codes returned by the C language APIs within the module is provided in the header files and the API documentation. Messages are also documented in the API documentation.

Data inputs/outputs are provided through the memory used for mailbox and IPC
Control input which controls the module's operation is provided through the mailbox by the Device OS' kernel and to applications located within the sepOS execution environment through IPC.

Status output is provided in return codes and through messages returned via the mailbox or the IPC. Documentation for each service invocation lists possible return codes. A complete list of all return codes returned by the C language APIs within the module is provided in the header files and the API documentation. Messages are also documented in the API documentation.
